description_long: This object is a simple, brown guard rail constructed from two horizontal rails supported by two vertical posts. The rails are parallel but slightly offset, giving a subtle slant to the lower rail. The posts are rectangular in cross-section and extend slightly above the top rail, providing a sturdy frame. The entire structure appears to be made of wood, with a matte finish and no visible hardware or decorative elements. The ends of the rails are blunt and squared off, with no flaring or additional treatment. There are no reflective markings or panels present, making it a basic, utilitarian barrier suitable for parks, sidewalks, or low-traffic urban areas.
description: A simple brown wooden guard rail with two horizontal rails and two vertical posts, featuring a matte finish and blunt ends.
description_view_0: Front view showing both horizontal rails and the two supporting vertical posts, with the lower rail slightly slanted.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile of the rails and posts, with minimal visible detail.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the rails and posts with the same slant and blunt ends.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the structure.
